description: S29 block/unblock ENFORCEMENT (needs echo_live): block the echo peer, then send a unique-nonce text. The peer normally mirrors it back inbound — with enforcement that inbound copy must be SUPPRESSED at the call site (no history, no unread, no _messages UI stream, no receipt) while the self-send still persists. Asserts blockedUsers contains the peer, the SELF copy count==1, and the INBOUND copy count==0 (enforcement on BOTH hybrid paths: the 4 FfiChatService inbound call sites + the binary-replacement hook). A WARMUP step first establishes the DHT route (reviewer HIGH #4) so the blocked echo arrives-and-is-dropped well within the 8s window — before the final unblock, closing the late-echo race. Then unblocks so other echo gates aren't left with a blocked peer (self-cleans; step 1 also unblocks defensively). Block is a LOCAL receive-side hide; blocking does NOT stop the outbound send (isSelf is never filtered). Maps to feature S29.
